titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Jones letters to Harman Grisewood collection
titlestmt.author: Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday November 16, 2021 at 04:02

did.unittitle: David Jones letters to Harman Grisewood

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of 158 autograph letters, signed, from David Jones to Harman Grisewood, dating from 1930 to 1974. The letters are personal in nature and discuss literature, religion, and other topics the two men had in common.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Jones Letters to Harman Grisewood. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: David Jones (1895-1974)
bioghist.p: David Jones (1895-1974), English artist and author.

bioghist.head: Harman Grisewood (1908-1997)
bioghist.p: Harman Grisewood (1908-1997), English radio actor, radio and television executive, author, and literary executor for David Jones.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Purchased from Harman Grisewood on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1979.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault 287
In 2021, the collection received a baseline level finding aid. Folder titles were based on those assigned by Library staff at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter dated December 2, 1957, enclosed two copies of Landmarks and Voyages: Poetry Supplement (edited by Vernon Watkins, for the Poetry Book Society, Christmas 1957) containing an extract from Jones's poem The Wall .
